name: Translation Intent
summary: Protocol defining how a locale change is delivered to the UI from a UX standpoint — instant (all catalogs preloaded, synchronous switch), async (lazy-load the target catalog behind a loading state), or reload (full per-locale document load). Orthogonal to the Locale Intent: locale declares which locale; translation declares how switching to it feels.

switching.description: How a locale change manifests in the UI, trading bundle/payload size against switch latency and client-state preservation

description:
Switching Strategy

How changing the active locale is delivered to the user. This is a UX and runtime-cost decision, independent of which locale is active (that is the Locale Intent). The implementing block decides the mechanism; this axis declares the experience the application wants.

  • instant: All candidate catalogs are preloaded (bundled or prefetched) before the switch. Changing locale is synchronous — no network round-trip, no loading state, no navigation; client state is preserved. Best for a small number of locales or compact catalogs. Cost: upfront payload grows with every supported locale.
  • async: The target locale's catalog is lazy-loaded on demand when the user switches. A loading state bridges the fetch and the UI updates in place when it resolves; client state is preserved. Smallest initial payload. Cost: a network round-trip on first switch to a locale — design the loading affordance via the Loader Intent.
  • reload: The switch is delivered by a full document load — navigating to a per-locale URL (/fr/…) or a per-locale build (SSR / static). There is no in-place reactive update; the new locale arrives with a fresh document. Best for SEO/SSR and compile-time per-locale builds. Cost: a full navigation that discards transient client state.

Composition

Each strategy leans on a neighbouring standard rather than re-solving it:

  • instant → preload the candidate catalogs ahead of need; eagerness composes with the Prefetch Intent.
  • async → the catalog fetch surfaces a pending state via the Loader Intent.
  • reload → the per-locale document is delivered by URL-based locale routing (Router).

Interface Protocol

type TranslationSwitching = 'instant' | 'async' | 'reload';

interface TranslationIntent {
    // How a locale change is delivered to the UI
    switching: TranslationSwitching;
}
